title: viola tricolor subsp. erecta
intro: Viola tricolor subsp. erecta, also known as wild pansy or heartsease, is a flowering plant species in the pansy family, characterized by its small, delicate purple, yellow, and white flowers. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Viola tricolor subsp. erecta include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, Viola tricolor subsp. erecta is often combined with other herbs, such as thyme and lemon balm, to create teas, infusions, and salves that promote relaxation and overall well-being.
